Rule-Based Score Computation
Deterministic

The composite risk score is computed using a weighted average of 5 dimensions. Each dimension score (1–4) is derived from rule-based lookups — not AI. The formula is: Composite = (D1×w1 + D2×w2 + D3×w3 + D4×w4 + D5×w5) / (w1+w2+w3+w4+w5) × 25

Formal investigation cases are managed in Lifecycle

Use KYC Documents for onboarding Enhanced Review Requirements. Use this queue for formal post-onboarding or manually escalated investigation cases with SLA, findings, senior review, and outcome controls.

Existing onboarding-created investigation cases are not hidden in this release; they remain visible for governance continuity and are labelled as investigation cases rather than onboarding evidence requirements.

Loading backend role matrix...

Risk Scoring Model Configuration

Manage the 5 risk dimensions, their weights, sub-criteria, and classification thresholds. Changes apply to all new applications.

Classification Thresholds

Composite score boundaries that determine the risk level. Score is 0–100.

Country Risk Classifications

Countries are scored based on FATF and sanctions lists. Score mapping: FATF Black = 4, Sanctioned = 4, FATF Grey = 3, Standard = 2, Low-Risk = 1.

Business Sector Risk Scores

Each business sector is assigned a risk score from 1 (low) to 4 (very high).

Entity Type Risk Scores

Each entity type is assigned a risk score from 1 (low) to 4 (very high).

AI Document Verification Checks

Configure the automated verification rules run by the AI engine when documents are uploaded. Currently 0 checks active.

Entity Documents
Person (KYC) Documents

AI Agent Pipeline

Configure the 10 AI agents that run during the onboarding lifecycle. Enable/disable agents, edit their checks, or adjust their behaviour.

Agent Health Dashboard

Real-time quality control monitoring for all AI agents. Track accuracy, confidence, overrides, and drift.

Ongoing Review Schedule

Set the review frequency for each risk category. This triggers reminders when client reviews are due. Per FIAMLA 2002 / FIAMLR 2018.

Low Risk Clients
Simplified due diligence — periodic review
Review every months 3 years
Medium Risk Clients
Standard due diligence — regular review
Review every months 2 years
High Risk Clients
Enhanced due diligence — frequent review
Review every months 1 year
Very High Risk Clients
Enhanced due diligence — intensive monitoring
Review every months 6 months
Reminder logic: When a review date is due within 30 days, a notification will appear on the dashboard and be sent to the assigned compliance officer. Overdue reviews are flagged in red.

Regulatory References

FATF Public Statement — High-Risk Jurisdictions
Updated list of countries subject to FATF call for action (Blacklist) and countries under increased monitoring (Greylist).
Open ↗
🌐
FSC Mauritius — AML/CFT Guidelines
Financial Services Commission regulatory guidance for licensees.
Open ↗
🌐
Sumsub AML — Watchlist & PEP Screening
Configured screening provider used for stored sanctions/watchlist and PEP-related AML checks. Distinct adverse-media results are not persisted in the current screening report.
Open ↗
Risk Classification Thresholds
Low Risk: 0 — 29.9
Medium Risk: 30 — 49.9
High Risk: 50 — 69.9
Very High Risk: 70 — 100
Approval Requirements
All applications: Require compliance memo (generated, validated, supervisor-approved), live screening, and no flagged documents
Low / Medium Risk: Single officer approval (Admin, SCO, or CO)
High / Very High Risk: Dual approval by two different officers + pre-approval before KYC
All approvals are human-initiated. No auto-approval exists regardless of risk score.
Upload New Resource

Drag & drop files here, or click to browse

Accepted formats: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX (max 25MB)

AI Compliance Supervisor Dashboard Decision Support — Rules-Based

Multi-agent analysis pipeline — contradiction detection, confidence evaluation, and compliance rule checks. Agents use deterministic rules and heuristic scoring. Results inform officer review but do not directly gate approval. Approval gating is enforced by the memo supervisor (Generate Memo → Validate → Approve).
